name: Azure Key Vault (Secrets, Keys, Certs Python SDK)

auth.notes: azure-identity DefaultAzureCredential; Managed Identity in production

performance.notes: REST API; regional endpoint; caching recommended for hot secrets

agent_readiness.notes: Azure's managed secrets, keys, and certificate store. from azure.keyvault.secrets import SecretClient. from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential. client = SecretClient(vault_url='https://my-vault.vault.azure.net/', credential=DefaultAzureCredential()). secret = client.get_secret('db-password'). secret.value. client.set_secret('api-key', 'value'). client.delete_secret('old-key'). Versioned secrets. Soft delete. Purge protection. Key Vault References for App Service/Functions (no SDK needed). Async: from azure.keyvault.secrets.aio import SecretClient. Keys SDK: azure-keyvault-keys. Certs: azure-keyvault-certificates.

gotchas: Three separate packages: azure-keyvault-secrets, azure-keyvault-keys, azure-keyvault-certificates
Soft-deleted secrets must be purged before recreating with same name
Rate limits: 2000 secrets GET/10s — cache hot secrets locally
Managed Identity permissions must be explicitly granted in Key Vault access policies or RBAC
best_when: Storing and retrieving secrets for Azure-hosted applications
App Service Key Vault References (automatic injection without SDK)
avoid_when: Non-Azure environments (HashiCorp Vault or AWS Secrets Manager better)
Simple single-developer apps (env vars may suffice)
Secrets that change extremely frequently (rate limits apply)
summary: Azure Key Vault Python SDK for managed secrets, keys, and certificates. SecretClient(vault_url, credential=DefaultAzureCredential()).get_secret('name').value. Versioned secrets with soft-delete and purge protection. Rate limit: 2000 GET/10s — cache hot secrets. Three packages: azure-keyvault-secrets, -keys, -certificates. Async support. App Service Key Vault References inject secrets automatically without SDK calls. Use Managed Identity in production — no credentials in code.
